sample_subject_id_evidence: GEO matrix/SOFT exposes CCFA_Risk IDs in sample titles: CCFA_Risk_001 through CCFA_Risk_322; 322 ileal biopsy samples. GEO summary says 359 treatment-naive pediatric CD/UC/control cohort, but only 322 samples are in the series.
overlap_result: Same RISK family as DS040/DS041, but subject overlap with DS040/GSE117993 or DS041/GSE93624 is not publicly provable from GEO matrix/SOFT because the other rows do not expose CCFA_Risk IDs.
recommended_overlap_notes: RISK pediatric ileal RNA-seq row. GSE57945 exposes CCFA_Risk_001-CCFA_Risk_322 sample identifiers, but GSE117993 uses accession-local rectal titles such as 1_RISK_Rectal_cCD and GSE93624 uses Sample1-Sample245 without CCFA_Risk IDs. Public GEO matrix/SOFT metadata do not provide a direct subject-ID crosswalk, so do not count DS005 as independent from DS040/DS041 or as overlapping with them without external subject mapping.

sample_subject_id_evidence: GEO matrix/SOFT exposes rectal sample titles such as 1_RISK_Rectal_cCD through 190_RISK_Rectal_UC; 190 rectal biopsy samples. These appear to be accession-local sample numbers, not cross-study subject IDs.
overlap_result: Same RISK family as DS005/DS041, but direct subject overlap is not publicly provable from GEO matrix/SOFT. Numeric prefixes should not be matched to CCFA_Risk or Sample numbers as subject IDs.
recommended_overlap_notes: RISK pediatric rectal RNA-seq row. GSE117993 contains 190 rectal biopsy samples titled 1_RISK_Rectal_cCD through 190_RISK_Rectal_UC, with diagnosis/location/subtype metadata but no CCFA_Risk-style subject IDs. Same RISK family as DS005 and DS041, but public GEO matrix/SOFT metadata do not prove exact subject overlap or independence. Do not sum as an independent RISK cohort without subject-level reconciliation.

sample_subject_id_evidence: GEO matrix/SOFT exposes generic sample titles Sample1 through Sample245; 245 ileal biopsy samples: 210 treatment-naive pediatric Crohn disease patients and 35 non-IBD controls from RISK. No CCFA_Risk IDs are exposed.
overlap_result: Same RISK family as DS005/DS040, but direct subject overlap is not publicly provable from GEO matrix/SOFT. Generic Sample1-Sample245 titles cannot be safely crosswalked to DS005 CCFA_Risk IDs or DS040 rectal numeric titles.
recommended_overlap_notes: RISK pediatric ileal Crohn RNA-seq row. GSE93624 contains 245 ileal samples titled Sample1-Sample245 and GEO states 210 treatment-naive pediatric Crohn disease patients plus 35 non-IBD controls from RISK. Same RISK family as DS005 and DS040, but public GEO matrix/SOFT metadata do not expose a direct subject-ID crosswalk. Do not count as independent from DS005/DS040 without external subject mapping.
